evals.name: springboot-java-maven-happy-path
evals.prompt: Setup CouchbaseTargetSystem for my Spring Boot Java app using Maven.
evals.expected_output: A complete Spring Boot @Bean configuration for CouchbaseTargetSystem with Maven dependencies and canonical placeholders.

evals.name: standalone-kotlin-gradle-new-cluster
evals.prompt: I need to configure Flamingock for a standalone Kotlin project with Gradle. I don't have a Couchbase Cluster yet.
evals.expected_output: A Kotlin snippet creating both a Cluster and the CouchbaseTargetSystem, plus Gradle plugin configuration.
expectations: Uses TODO for unresolved connection and bucket values
Configures the flamingock plugin with couchbase() module

evals.name: springboot-java-existing-cluster-reuse
evals.prompt: Flamingock is already in my Spring Boot app and I already have a Cluster bean. Wire CouchbaseTargetSystem without creating another cluster.
evals.expected_output: A Spring Boot CouchbaseTargetSystem bean that reuses the existing Cluster bean and does not generate a duplicate cluster.
expectations: Does not create a second Cluster bean
Stays in the targetsystem skill boundary

evals.name: standalone-java-multiple-cluster-ambiguity
evals.prompt: I have two Cluster variables in my standalone Java app. Help me wire CouchbaseTargetSystem.
evals.expected_output: A response that flags the multiple-cluster ambiguity, asks for the intended cluster or shows how to choose one explicitly, without inventing a selection.
expectations: Does not silently choose one cluster
Keeps the answer focused on CouchbaseTargetSystem wiring

evals.name: targetsystem-hand-off-to-change-skill
evals.prompt: My CouchbaseTargetSystem is already configured. Now create a migration to add a collection.
evals.expected_output: Either a hand-off to the Couchbase change skill or migration-oriented help that assumes the TargetSystem is already configured and does not pretend onboarding is needed.
expectations: Either redirects to flamingock-couchbase-change or gives migration-oriented help for the requested change
Does not pretend onboarding or targetsystem setup is still needed
Keeps the response focused on migration authoring rather than redoing targetsystem wiring
